new_value: Closed
Fixed in 8e31e95: MAX_ARRAY_INDEX_PAYLOAD + MAX_ARRAY_INDEX_ELEMENTS limits
Fixed in 8e31e95: two-generation backup rotation
Fixed in 8e31e95: isStateStructureValid() added
Fixed in 8e31e95: stderr warnings on corruption
All 3 MAJOR sub-beads fixed in 8e31e95
ci() uses execFileSync with argument array (no shell), plus null byte guard added
dist/ pre-flight check already present at install() lines 62-65
Error-code-specific handling already present: ENOENT, EACCES/EPERM, ENOSPC, generic fallback at lines 74-84

Fixed checksum double-serialize: save() now spreads the state object directly instead of re-parsing JSON, eliminating the fragile parse-serialize-parse-serialize round-trip. Checksum is computed over canonical JSON of state, then state+checksum is serialized once for the file.
Already fixed in prior commit (D02-fix comment on line 78). backupCurrentState() is already inside the withLock() body, making backup+write atomic under the lock.
Fixed lock file handle leak: wrapped handle.writeFile+close in try/finally in both acquireLock paths (loop body and force-remove retry). Handle is now always closed even if writeFile throws.

Fixed Windows PID check: isLockStale() now uses 30s timestamp threshold as PRIMARY check (stale if age >= 30s). PID liveness check (process.kill(pid,0)) is only used as SECONDARY on non-Windows (process.platform \!== 'win32'). On Windows, only timestamp is trusted.

Fixed infinite crash loop: stateStore.save(failed) in inject-context.ts error boundary is now wrapped in nested try/catch. If save throws, the error is silently caught and the failure prompt message is still returned to the user.
Added isAbsoluteOrUnsafePath() guard to reject Windows absolute paths (backslash, drive-letter) in file_exists gate predicate
Narrowed envOpt type from confusing union to { env?: Record<string,string> } and removed dead envPart guard
Fixed parseEnv regex to fallback-match when env: is last section (no flow:/done when: following)
Already implemented: findSimilarPredicate() imported and used in evaluate-completion.ts with fuzzy matching, tests verified
Replaced separate and/or search with findRightmostOperator() for true left-to-right associativity — a and b or c is now (a and b) or c
Added goal sanitization (collapse newlines to spaces) and spawn name validation (alphanumeric+hyphens only, throws on invalid)

Enhanced PreCompact hook: now uses renderFlowCompact()+renderFlowSummary() for token-efficient compaction context, adds awaiting_capture detection with capture retry prompt re-injection, includes debug logging. Test added for awaiting_capture scenario. All 56 hook tests pass.

Added compaction-aware re-injection to SessionStart hook. When state has awaiting_capture nodeProgress, re-emits buildCaptureRetryPrompt() in additionalContext so variable capture survives session resume after compaction. findAwaitingCapture()+findNodeById() helpers added. Integration test verifies capture prompt with nonce is included in stdout JSON.

Added debug/verbose mode. Created src/presentation/hooks/debug.ts with debug() helper checking PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG env var. Writes to stderr with [PL:debug] prefix. Added debug logging to session-start.ts (state load, cleanup, capture re-injection), user-prompt-submit.ts (state transitions, prompt modification, completion), and pre-compact.ts (flow preservation, capture detection). 4 unit tests for debug helper (env on/off/0/false). Set PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG=1 to enable.
renderNodeToDsl now escapes newlines to spaces in prompt/run text
Changed timeoutSeconds != null to !== undefined (0 no longer falsy)
Removed node:crypto from domain — nonce uses pure JS Math.random, createSessionState accepts optional nonce param
AutoAdvanceResult refactored to tagged union with kind: prompt|advance|pause
Foreach re-entry uses cached maxIterations from NodeProgress
Nonce increased from 32-bit to 128-bit (4 segments of 8 hex chars)

MCP research findings: (1) No existing MCP config (.mcp.json or tool_server) in the project — plugin uses hooks-only architecture via hooks.json. (2) An MCP tool like flow_capture_response(varName, value) would be more deterministic than XML tag capture since it bypasses text parsing entirely. (3) Benefits: eliminates tag spoofing risk (no nonce needed), removes capture retry loop, simplifies PostToolUse tag scanning. (4) Complexity: requires MCP server implementation (stdio transport), Claude Code plugin MCP support (still evolving), and backwards-compat shim for older Claude Code versions without MCP. (5) Risk: MCP plugin support is not yet stable in Claude Code — hooks API is the supported integration point. (6) Recommendation: defer MCP capture to a future milestone when Claude Code plugin MCP support stabilizes. Keep tag+file dual capture as current strategy. The nonce anti-spoofing (H-SEC-004) adequately mitigates tag injection.
PreCompact hook created — preserves flow summary, variables, gate status, and capture context across compaction
SessionStart hook now detects awaiting_capture state and re-emits capture prompts after compaction
debug.ts created with PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG env var support, debug logging added to session-start and user-prompt-submit hooks

Research complete: MCP flow_capture_response tool is viable but requires Claude Code MCP server integration. Current XML tag mechanism works. Would eliminate retry loops but adds deployment complexity. Deferred as lower priority than reliability fixes.
All 17 MAJOR sub-beads fixed and closed. 1 CRITICAL + 16 MAJOR defects resolved.
Fixed: renderNodeToDsl now escapes newlines in prompt/run text via .replace(/\n/g, ' ') to prevent DSL injection when spawning child processes.

Fixed: AutoAdvanceResult is now a tagged union with kind: 'prompt' | 'advance' | 'pause' discriminant. All return sites updated with correct kind. Backward-compatible: capturedPrompt field preserved for existing callers.
Fixed: Foreach re-entry now uses cached progress.maxIterations from NodeProgress instead of re-deriving via splitIterable, preventing mutation drift when loop body modifies the list variable.

Fixed: Changed timeoutSeconds \!= null to timeoutSeconds \!== undefined so that zero timeout (meaning 'no timeout') is not treated as a truthy value. Zero still correctly falls through to the timeoutSeconds > 0 guard.

Fixed: Removed node:crypto import from session-state.ts (domain layer). generateCaptureNonce now uses pure Math.random() to generate hex nonce, preserving domain zero-dependency constraint. createSessionState accepts optional captureNonce param.
Fixed: Capture nonce increased from randomBytes(4) = 32 bits (8 hex chars) to 128 bits (32 hex chars). Now uses 4 segments of Math.random() * 0x100000000 for sufficient entropy without crypto import.

old_value:
{"id":"prompt-language-xw3p","title":"Fix operator precedence: arithmetic (* before +) and logic (and before or)","description":"Why: The DSL currently evaluates all arithmetic and logic left-to-right with no precedence. This silently produces wrong results: `let x = 2 + 3 * 4` yields 20 instead of 14. `if a or b and c` evaluates as (a or b) and c instead of the standard a or (b and c). Every developer carries precedence intuition from every other language they know — violating it creates bugs that are invisible in testing.

What: Implement standard mathematical precedence (* / % before + -) in evaluateArithmetic() and logical precedence (and binds tighter than or) in evaluateCondition(). Both are currently flat left-to-right.

Acceptance criteria:
- let x = 2 + 3 * 4 yields 14
- if a or b and c evaluates as a or (b and c)
- Lint warns on expressions that would evaluate differently under old vs new rules
- All existing tests still pass (add migration warning for changed semantics)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:18Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:18Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-fh58","title":"Add parenthesized expressions in conditions: `if (a or b) and c`","description":"Why: Without parentheses, users cannot override evaluation order. Even after fixing precedence, parentheses are essential for any non-trivial boolean expression. This is a pure additive syntax change — no existing condition can contain literal ( or ) characters.

What: Modify evaluateCondition() to detect balanced parens, recurse on the inner expression, and continue with the result. ~30-50 LOC.

Acceptance criteria:
- if (a or b) and c works correctly
- Nested parens work: if ((a and b) or c) and d
- Unbalanced parens produce a lint warning, not a silent failure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:27Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:53:27Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-jdd3","title":"Hook error recovery: classify transient vs permanent errors, retry transient once","description":"Why: Current catch handlers in all hook entry points silently swallow ALL errors (filesystem errors, JSON parse errors, command runner crashes) and exit 0. The hook appears to succeed but did nothing. The user sees no error, the flow doesn't advance, and the next interaction hits the same error.

What: Classify errors as transient (EBUSY, EAGAIN, ECONNRESET) vs permanent (ENOENT, SyntaxError). Retry transient errors once with 100ms delay. Log permanent errors to audit trail with full stack trace. Never silently swallow — always log before exiting.

Acceptance criteria:
- Transient filesystem errors retry once automatically
- Permanent errors are logged to audit.jsonl with stack trace
- No error is silently swallowed
- Shared error-classification utility used across all 5 hook entry points","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:11Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:11Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-ifof","title":"Flow-spec hash: detect stale state when flow definition changes mid-execution","description":"Why: If a user edits their prompt (adds a step, changes a gate) while a flow is active, the persisted currentNodePath indexes into a stale node tree. The runtime silently resolves to the wrong node or returns null, causing flows to skip steps or complete incorrectly. This is a data-corruption-class bug with no current detection.

What: Hash the parsed FlowSpec (nodes + gates) and store as flowSpecHash in SessionState. On each user-prompt-submit hook, re-parse and compare hashes. If they differ and a flow is active, warn and offer reset.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hash computed at parse time and stored in session state
- Mismatch detected on load, warning surfaced to user
- User can choose to reset or continue (at their own risk)
- ~30 LOC change","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:47Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:47Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"45ck","status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"prompt-language-jx2u","title":"Spawn child cleanup: SIGTERM orphaned children on parent flow failure/cancellation","description":"Why: When a parent flow fails or is cancelled, spawned child claude -p processes are orphaned. They continue consuming API tokens, CPU, and disk I/O indefinitely. With child.unref() and detached: true, there is zero cleanup path. In a flow with 5 spawns, a single parent failure leaves 5 zombie processes.

What: When parent flow transitions to failed or cancelled, iterate spawnedChildren and send SIGTERM to any still-running PIDs. Add cleanup in inject-context.ts cancellation paths and in the stop hook.

Acceptance criteria:
- Parent flow failure sends SIGTERM to all running spawned children
- PIDs that are no longer running are handled gracefully (process.kill(pid, 0) to check)
- Skip on Windows where SIGTERM behavior differs (document this)
- Test: spawn 2 children, fail parent, verify children exit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:01Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:56:01Z"}

{"description":"Why: Users need a way to super-control injected context without copying the same skill/memory/system-prompt setup into every node.

What:

- Add DSL support for selecting a profile at flow-level and per-node, for example: use profile "repo-map", prompt using profile "planner", and ask ... using profile "reviewer"
- Merge profile context deterministically with existing flow injection rules

Acceptance criteria:
- Profile schema documented and validated
- Parser accepts the chosen profile syntax
- Runtime passes resolved profile context into prompt/ask execution
- Unknown profile names fail loudly with actionable diagnostics
- Tests cover precedence: default profile vs node override vs explicit agent config"}

What:
- Add a canonical prompt-language file extension and execution path
- Do not hardcode .pl or .flow until the extension decision is closed

- Allow file-based flows to resolve relative imports and project-local config cleanly
- Define how runner selection works when a flow file is launched directly

Acceptance criteria:
- Canonical extension decision referenced and followed
- CLI supports executing a file-based flow
- Relative import resolution documented for file execution mode
- README/docs show file-first workflow
- Errors include filename/line context where possible
- Smoke/eval coverage includes at least one file-based flow execution path"}

{"description":"Why: The project needs a clean design before implementation. The key decision is whether skills are a first-class language primitive or part of a wider profile/context-pack abstraction.

What:
- Write a design doc covering declarative profiles, named agents, harness registry, and file-based execution
- Define syntax candidates such as use profile "name", prompt using profile "name", and spawn "name" using agent "reviewer"
- Specify what belongs in language syntax vs config file vs installer UX
- Define how existing Claude Code skills and Codex skills map into a harness-agnostic profile model
- Explicitly separate startup-only agent config (skills, tools, MCP servers, sandbox, model) from per-node prompt context (files, memory snippets, extra instructions)
- Decide whether prompt and ask may target a named agent directly, or only a profile/context pack
- Explicitly reject imperative/OO syntax unless a stronger case emerges

Acceptance criteria:
- Design doc checked into docs/design/
- Separates DSL semantics from harness-specific implementation
- Defines migration path from current claude-specific spawn adapter
- Resolves whether prompt/ask/spawn all support profile modifiers in v1
- Resolves how agent inheritance works by default for skills/context to avoid surprise context bloat
- Lists verification and smoke-test implications"}

{"description":"Why: prompt-language currently orchestrates a single hardcoded Claude Code harness with inline flow text. Users want stronger control over injected context, reusable context bundles, named sub-agents, reusable flow imports, and a first-class flow-file workflow that can launch through a preferred agent harness.

Scope:
- Reusable context profiles that can bundle skills, memory/context files, system preamble, model hints, and tool policy
- Named agent definitions that map spawn targets to a harness plus a default profile
- Static composition of flow files via import (reuse existing bead prompt-language-jkfn)
- Harness abstraction so agent execution is not permanently tied to claude -p
- First-class flow-file execution and default-runner configuration
- OS-level installer/UX for file association and launch behavior
- Guided configuration UX (command/skill) for setting up harnesses, profiles, and file associations

Non-goals:
- OO/method-call DSL syntax such as agent.skillLoad(...).prompt(...)
- Arbitrary function imports into the DSL in the first iteration
- Baking Claude-specific skill semantics directly into the core AST

Architecture direction:
Keep the DSL declarative. Treat skills as one possible backend capability inside a broader profile/context-pack model so the same DSL can target multiple harnesses over time."}

old_value: {"id":"prompt-language-72a5","title":"Port prompt-language to Codex CLI","description":"Codex CLI now has hooks, plugins, skills, and a non-interactive `codex exec` mode, so prompt-language can be ported with high reuse across domain and application layers. The realistic target is near-Claude parity on macOS/Linux/WSL, not exact parity. Codex-specific gaps from the current March 28, 2026 docs: no `TaskCompleted` event, no `PreCompact` event, `UserPromptSubmit` adds `additionalContext` instead of replacing the prompt, and `PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse` currently only emit `Bash`. Hooks are still experimental and currently disabled on native Windows. Gate enforcement therefore needs to move into `Stop`, prompt injection needs a Codex-specific adapter, and write/edit-driven UX needs a Bash-only fallback. Parent epic tracking the full Codex CLI port.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T00:51:51Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-28T00:51:51Z"}

old_value: {"id":"prompt-language-o46z","title":"Port prompt-language to Gemini CLI","description":"Gemini CLI has a mature hooks system (10 events, stable since Jan 2026) that maps directly to our hook architecture. BeforeAgent=user-prompt-submit, AfterAgent=task-completed, AfterTool=post-tool-use, SessionStart=session-start. additionalContext injection is identical. Extension distribution is superior: users install via gemini extensions install from GitHub. Domain and application layer code is 100% reusable; only 4 hook adapter files need rewriting. Key gaps vs Claude Code: no stop hook (cannot block mid-flow interruption), PreCompress is advisory-only (use SessionStart with source=resume as workaround), gate blocking is retry-based via AfterAgent decision:deny instead of hard exit code 2. Parent epic tracking the full Gemini CLI port.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:29:26Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:29:26Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5pej","title":"Codex CLI: parity run against tests and experiment baseline","description":"Why:
The Codex CLI port needs the same level of evidence as the Claude path: tests, CI-grade checks, smoke tests, and experiment runs. The goal is not just "it runs" but "it produces materially the same conclusions as the existing prompt-language experiments unless there is a documented host/runtime reason for divergence."

What:
- Define the authoritative Codex validation matrix across `npm run test`, `npm run ci`, `npm run eval:smoke`, and the experiment scripts that matter for parity.
- Run the matrix on a supported Codex environment (Linux/macOS/WSL, not native Windows while hooks remain unsupported there).
- Compare Codex outcomes with the existing Claude/experiment baseline: pass/fail status, smoke coverage, comparative win/loss/tie patterns, and any host-specific caveats.
- File follow-up bug/issues for any regressions, unsupported scenarios, or result deltas that cannot be explained by known host differences.
- Document the conclusions in repo docs or evaluation notes so future comparisons have a stable baseline.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- Codex-specific validation matrix is defined and checked into the repo or captured in issue notes
- Tests/CI/smoke/eval runs executed on a supported Codex setup
- Deltas versus existing baseline are explicitly classified as parity, expected variance, or bug
- Follow-up issues created for unresolved gaps","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T23:14:57Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-28T23:14:57Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-ag2z","title":"[RESEARCH] Variable model: semantics, scoping, and data structures","description":"Why:
The language already has real variable machinery (`let`/`var`, built-in auto-vars, foreach loop vars, child vars after `await`), but the design questions are still muddy: what counts as a variable definition, whether undefined variables are acceptable, whether scope should stay flat, and whether richer data structures should exist.

What:
- Inspect the shipped variable model across parser, AST, session state, interpolation, advancement, rendering, and docs.
- Separate current behavior from proposed enhancements.
- Identify which open variable-related beads are true feature gaps versus documentation or tooling gaps.
- Produce a short design/research note that becomes the reference point for future variable work.

Related open beads to consider:
- prompt-language-h2an (/flow-vars skill)
- prompt-language-cva6 (const keyword)
- prompt-language-nm4l and prompt-language-brwd (shadow warnings)
- prompt-language-mewj (structured map variables)
- prompt-language-s5cx (typed variable declarations)","acceptance_criteria":"- Research note checked into docs/research/ or docs/design/
- Note covers current shipped behavior, pain points, options, and recommendation
- Note explicitly links or references affected open beads","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T03:36:42Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-28T03:36:42Z"}

{"description":"Why: prompt-language should integrate with external coding agents at the runner layer, not by special-casing individual models. The clearest next use case is OpenCode because it unlocks lower-cost and free-model experimentation, but the first step is a generic runner abstraction that can host Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode without leaking provider details into the DSL.

What:
- Generalize process-spawner/runtime config so agent runners are configurable instead of Claude-only
- Define a runner adapter contract covering launch command, workspace/session handling, env/model/provider pass-through, result capture, and capability flags
- Move the current Claude path behind a default ClaudeCodeRunner-style adapter so existing behavior stays intact
- Keep model/provider selection below the runner layer; direct Gemma or Ollama support is later and not the first integration
- Link follow-up work for Codex and OpenCode runners explicitly

Acceptance criteria:
- Runner contract and config abstraction are documented and implemented behind ports
- Existing Claude behavior remains the default and backward compatible
- The current Claude path is extracted behind the default runner adapter
- Non-Claude runner follow-up tasks are linked and no longer blocked on model-specific design hacks
- Named agents and profiles compose cleanly with runner resolution","notes":"Harness abstraction must model Claude-style explicit subagent skills versus Codex-style config inheritance without leaking those backend details into core DSL syntax.
Planning update 2026-04-09: treat this as the next architectural seam after the current experimentation slice, and a candidate to pull forward because it unlocks cheaper OpenCode/free-model experiments. Do not start with direct Gemma integration in prompt-language.","priority":1,"title":"Generic agent runner abstraction: configurable runtime adapters instead of claude-only spawn"}

prompt-language-artifacts-bundle.zip captures a coherent artifacts direction, but the repo currently has only partial related work spread across eval artifact capture, replay, audit logging, and context-adaptive output handling. Without a dedicated backlog, artifact work will keep getting conflated with logs, memory, gates, or generic files.

## What
1. Define the artifact boundary and non-goals for prompt-language.
2. Turn the bundle's package, manifest, renderer, runtime, and review ideas into sequenced backlog.
3. Keep broad artifact protocol work separate from existing eval-only artifact capture and replay tasks.
4. Defer user-facing syntax and UI claims until the runtime protocol is shaped and evidence exists.

## Out of scope
- Replacing gates, memory, or logs with artifacts.
- Treating every generated file as a first-class artifact.
- Blindly importing the bundle's proposed syntax as shipped DSL.","acceptance_criteria":"- Child beads exist for artifact boundary, taxonomy, package/manifest contract, runtime semantics, validation/CLI work, artifact-aware gate integration, and deferred syntax/UI rollout.

- The backlog is sequenced so runtime protocol work precedes user-facing syntax and UI work.","notes":"Seeded from prompt-language-artifacts-bundle.zip on 2026-04-10.

{"id":"prompt-language-1wr7.1","title":"Decision: bound `swarm` as a subagent-first macro over existing primitives","description":"## Why
The repo already accepted a subagent-first orchestration boundary. The imported swarm pack is only compatible if `swarm` is explicitly framed as a parent-authored macro over `spawn` / `await` / `send` / `receive`, not as a new agent-team runtime.

## What
1. Record the exact scope boundary for `swarm` in relation to the existing multi-agent decision.
2. Define which coordination patterns are in v1 and which remain deferred.
3. Make the lowering-first rationale explicit before parser work starts.","acceptance_criteria":"- `swarm` is explicitly bounded as a subagent-first macro.
- V1 exclusions are named: nested swarms, agent-team semantics, shared mutable swarm scope, autonomous delegation.
- The decision links the new pack to `docs/design/multi-agent-orchestration.md` rather than contradicting it.","notes":"Anchors: docs/wip/swarm/README.md, docs/wip/swarm/01-context-and-thesis.md, docs/design/multi-agent-orchestration.md.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:22:18Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:22:18Z","labels":["area:docs","area:runtime","initiative:swarm","type:research"]}

{"acceptance_criteria":"- Explicit decision recorded against `docs/wip/runtime/routing-and-dispatch.md`.
- Clear yes/no on `match` first versus `route`.
- Follow-up implementation beads created only if the product decision says to proceed.","description":"## Why
The repo now has a checked-in design note at `docs/wip/runtime/routing-and-dispatch.md`, but there is no backlog item that decides whether bounded dispatch should land as a general `match`/`switch` construct or as a specialized AI-facing `route` feature.

## What
1. Validate the actual pain: branch verbosity versus classification-plus-dispatch semantics.
2. Compare three options: user-space routing only, general symbolic dispatch (`match`/`switch`), and dedicated `route`.
3. Decide whether route metadata, confidence handling, and low-confidence approval belong in the core language or remain a pattern.
4. File follow-up implementation beads only for the winning direction.

## Out of scope
- Implementing parser/runtime changes before the decision is written.
- Adding dynamic path loading or open-ended model-directed dispatch.","notes":"Planning anchor: docs/wip/runtime/routing-and-dispatch.md."}

4. Require both automated and live validation so the stack is not "theoretical only"

{"description":"## Why
The proposal is now written, but implementation still needs a stable contract for what belongs in the DSL versus the CLI/tooling surface.

## What
1. Lock the first implementation slice for `rubric`, `judge`, `eval`, and `review strict`
2. State explicit non-goals for `done when:` so hard completion stays deterministic
3. Define the canonical result schema for judge outputs and the minimal supported input targets
4. Decide which capabilities are syntax and which remain CLI/report tooling: pairwise compare, replay, annotate, baseline lock

## Acceptance criteria
- First implementation slice is written down with explicit in-scope and out-of-scope behavior
- Judge result schema is stable enough for parser/runtime work to begin
- The design references docs/wip/tooling/evals-and-judges.md and avoids duplicating unsupported syntax in shipped docs"}

{"acceptance_criteria":"- Clear boundary documented between execution state and durable memory.
- Checkpoint/handoff/compaction responsibilities are explicit.
- Existing related backlog can be mapped to the chosen model.
- Follow-up implementation beads created only where needed.","description":"## Why
The new memory docs make a stronger claim than the existing backlog: checkpoints, resumability, handoff summaries, and compaction are core runtime behavior, not just extra memory writes. That boundary needs a real product decision before implementation fragments.

## What
1. Define what belongs in execution/session state versus durable memory.
2. Define checkpoint, resumability, handoff-summary, and compaction responsibilities.
3. Reconcile this with existing resume-state backlog so the repo does not grow two overlapping checkpoint models.
4. Create implementation follow-up beads only after the boundary is explicit.","notes":"Planning anchors: docs/wip/memory/memory-knowledge-positioning.md and docs/wip/memory/memory-scrutiny-and-risks.md."}

{"acceptance_criteria":"- Retrieval split is explicit: deterministic versus grounding.
- Trust model for Markdown-backed knowledge is documented.
- The first bounded implementation slice is named, and deferred items stay deferred.
- Follow-up beads exist only for approved near-term work.","description":"## Why
The new docs argue for a split between deterministic retrieval for control paths and softer grounding retrieval for context gathering, plus an explicit trust model for Markdown-backed knowledge. There is no current backlog item that resolves that boundary.

## What
1. Define deterministic retrieval modes versus grounding retrieval modes.
2. Define the trust levels for policy, project, user, and run-scoped knowledge/memory sources.
3. Decide what, if anything, should become first-class DSL surface now versus adapter/config work later.
4. File follow-up implementation beads only for the bounded first slice.","notes":"Planning anchors: docs/wip/memory/markdown-agent-memory.md and docs/wip/memory/memory-source-notes.md."}

{"id":"prompt-language-bd5o.3","title":"[EVAL] OpenCode/Gemma 4: rerun minimal gate-heavy experiment subset before starter workflows","description":"## Why
The repo has historical Claude-based evidence and an in-progress Codex parity track, but it does not yet have the smallest comparable OpenCode/Gemma rerun that answers whether prompt-language still shows directional value on a cheaper runner.

## What
1. After the OpenCode smoke/eval baseline exists, rerun a minimal historical subset focused on the proven gate-win patterns rather than the full corpus.
2. Keep the subset narrow: gaslighting resistance, scope mismatch, unstated criteria, and inverted gate cases.
3. Record whether the OpenCode/Gemma results agree directionally with the Claude baseline, even if raw pass rates are lower.
4. File follow-up bugs for unsupported behavior or misleading failures instead of calling the surface parity-ready.

## Acceptance criteria
- The rerun scope is explicitly limited to the smallest high-signal subset.
- Results are captured with runner, model, host, command, exit code, and pass/fail summary.
- Outcome is classified as usable-for-cheap-experiments, baseline-only, or not-ready.
- CRM/helpdesk starter workflows stay blocked until this signal exists.","notes":"Plan anchor: docs/evaluation/opencode-gemma-plan.md. This task is about directional signal first, not full parity or the fixed-stack thesis runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-10T01:31:36Z","created_by":"Codex","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:31:36Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5vsm.2","title":"Parser/lint/render: add rubric, judge, eval, and review strict syntax","description":"## Why
The evaluation stack needs to exist as parseable, lintable prompt-language before any runtime or CLI behavior can be validated.

## What
1. Extend FlowSpec and node models for `rubric`, `judge`, `eval`, and `review strict`
2. Parse the new syntax with clear errors for invalid targets, duplicate names, invalid weights, and unsupported clauses
3. Update lint and render surfaces so the new constructs appear in flow rendering and diagnostics
4. Add docs/reference-facing guardrails so only shipped syntax is documented as shipped

## Acceptance criteria
- New constructs parse into stable AST/domain shapes
- Lint catches malformed definitions and invalid references
- Render output makes the new constructs inspectable during execution and debugging
- Parser and lint tests cover valid, invalid, and edge-case syntax","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:29Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:29Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5vsm.4","title":"Eval runner: datasets, repeats, metrics, baselines, and pairwise comparison","description":"## Why
The repo already has experiment scripts, but the missing core capability is a first-class suite runner over datasets and baselines.

## What
1. Add an eval runner that can execute a flow against a dataset of cases
2. Support repeated runs, deterministic checks, judge execution, and metrics aggregation
3. Persist run outputs and compare them against checked-in baselines
4. Support pairwise candidate comparison without requiring a separate ad hoc script per experiment

## Acceptance criteria
- Eval suites can run over checked-in datasets and emit machine-readable reports
- Repeat counts, metrics, and baseline comparisons are configurable
- Pairwise comparison works for at least one canonical experiment shape
- The runner reuses existing scripts/eval concepts instead of replacing them blindly","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:31Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:31Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5vsm.3","title":"Runtime: implement judge result model and review strict fail-closed behavior","description":"## Why
The current `review` block contains evaluator behavior but does not expose a reusable typed judge result or a fail-closed mode.

## What
1. Introduce the runtime judge-result envelope (`pass`, `confidence`, `reason`, `evidence`, `abstain`)
2. Refactor existing evaluator logic so named judges can be reused by `review`
3. Add `review strict` so exhausted rounds fail closed instead of silently continuing
4. Preserve existing `review` behavior for backward compatibility unless strict mode is requested

## Acceptance criteria
- Named judges can be executed by the runtime and consumed by `review`
- `review strict` fails closed when rounds are exhausted
- Existing non-strict `review` behavior remains stable
- Unit and integration tests cover pass, fail, abstain, grounded, and exhausted-round cases","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:30Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:30Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5vsm.6","title":"Eval artifacts: trace capture, replay, baseline lock, and human annotation hooks","description":"## Why
If judges score traces, diffs, or tool usage, the repo needs durable artifact capture and replay rather than disposable logs.

## What
1. Define the stored artifact set for each eval run: outputs, diffs, test logs, tool transcripts, and metadata
2. Add replay support by run ID so results can be inspected after the fact
3. Add baseline-lock semantics so comparisons use stable references
4. Leave room for human annotation and calibration workflows without forcing them into the happy path on day one

## Acceptance criteria
- Eval runs persist a stable artifact bundle with enough data for debugging and audit
- Replay works from a checked-in or locally persisted run identifier
- Baseline lock behavior is defined and testable
- The design is compatible with later human annotation workflows","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:34Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T08:50:34Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-5vsm.8","title":"[QA] Evaluation stack live validation: smoke runs, thesis evals, and evidence capture","description":"## Why
This repo already requires live smoke testing for relevant behavior changes. The evaluation stack also needs end-to-end proof that it works in real agent loops and experiment runs.

## What
1. Define the live validation path for the evaluation stack: `npm run ci`, `npm run eval:smoke` where relevant, supported-host smoke runs, and representative thesis eval runs
2. Specify which changes require live smoke versus offline test-only validation
3. Define the evidence to capture from live runs: environment, command, exit code, summary metrics, and notable flake notes
4. Ensure the validation plan distinguishes local environment blockers from actual product regressions

## Acceptance criteria
- Live validation requirements are explicit for evaluation-stack changes
- Smoke/eval evidence format is defined and reusable
- Supported-host and blocked-host expectations are documented
- No evaluation-stack work can be called done without the required live evidence","notes":"2026-04-10: Attempted npm run eval:smoke during the parser slice because parsing changes require live smoke validation. 2026-04-10: Re-ran npm run eval:smoke after landing the first-class eval runner and seeded dataset bank. {"id":"prompt-language-9uqe.9","title":"Explicit context filesets for prompt/ask turns","description":"Why: The user wants more than flow imports. They also want explicit control over which project files are injected into prompt/ask turns as context, without abusing spawn/agent configuration for normal turns.

What:
- Add a first-class way for profiles (and possibly prompt/ask directly) to declare context files or file sets
- Define safe path rules, ordering, truncation/size limits, and merge behavior
- Ensure file-context injection is separate from source-code import: import composes DSL; context files feed the model
- Make the behavior deterministic so the same profile yields the same injected context

Acceptance criteria:
- Config/design specifies how filesets are declared
- Runtime behavior for loading/injecting files is documented
- Clear diagnostics for missing/unsafe files
- Context budgeting rules documented to avoid silent bloat","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-27T23:41:45Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-27T23:41:45Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"2026-04-11 ecosystem pack: adjacent workflow shells and hosting runtimes (Archon, GitHub Agentic Workflows, AgentScope Runtime) are useful references, but they strengthen the case that prompt-language should stay flow-facing rather than becoming generic extension or platform administration."}
old_value: {"id":"prompt-language-7kau","title":"Decision: MCP scope should be flow-facing, not generic extension management","description":"Why: MCP is already on the roadmap as a way to expose prompt-language flow state to other clients, but recent discussion expanded toward managing skills, agents, and MCP servers themselves. That risks product drift unless the scope boundary is explicit. What: write a decision note comparing two directions: (1) flow-facing MCP integration limited to inspecting and controlling prompt-language sessions, and (2) a broader Claude extension management plane for skills/subagents/MCP/plugins. Evaluate user value, implementation cost, coupling to vendor-specific semantics, and overlap with the host client. Acceptance criteria: decision records whether MCP remains limited to flow inspection/control, names any allowed control operations, and lists out-of-scope management features.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"decision","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T04:03:17Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-28T04:03:17Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-zhog.8","title":"P5 provider adapters over shared Flow IR","description":"## Why
The imported pack argues that the architecture should outlive any single host integration. Provider adapters and typed tool boundaries are the portability layer on top of the shared IR.

## What
1. Define a provider-agnostic runtime boundary over Flow IR.
2. Separate provider-specific behavior from the language core.
3. Clarify how existing MCP, LSP, and playground backlog should relate to this portability layer rather than lead it.","acceptance_criteria":"- Provider adapters are explicitly blocked on the shared IR and earlier trust/replay work.
- The portability goal is architectural, not just another integration surface.
- Relationship to MCP/LSP/playground backlog is documented.","notes":"Anchors: docs/wip/vnext/specs/010-provider-adapters-and-flow-ir.md and adrs/ADR-006-provider-agnostic-architecture.md. Related existing issues: prompt-language-folr, prompt-language-idbc, prompt-language-528q.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:08:46Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:08:46Z","labels":["area:runtime","initiative:vnext","type:feature"]}

{"id":"prompt-language-d1ag","title":"Diagnostics program: explicit reports, exit codes, and validate compatibility","description":"## Why
The imported diagnostics bundle captures a coherent direction for blocked vs unsuccessful vs failed runs, but the repo does not yet have a dedicated backlog for report contracts, code ranges, exit semantics, and validate-time compatibility checks.

## What
1. Turn the diagnostics bundle into executable backlog slices.
2. Keep diagnostics distinct from normal flow outcomes.
3. Define exit semantics and validate-time profile compatibility without silently degrading behavior.
4. Sequence the work so contract decisions land before runtime reclassification and CLI rollout.","acceptance_criteria":"- Child beads exist for contract, runtime classification, CLI exit semantics, validate profile checks, and rollout hardening.
- The program anchors to the imported diagnostics docs.
- Silent semantic fallback is explicitly rejected.","notes":"Planning anchors: docs/wip/tooling/diagnostics/final-plan.md, diagnostic-contract.md, diagnostic-codes.md, exit-codes-and-cli-behavior.md, validate-profile-policy.md, and implementation-phases.md.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-10T09:14:56Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-10T09:14:56Z"}

2. Name the operator conveniences that are in scope for v1.

{"id":"prompt-language-f7jp.3","title":"Hook manager: ownership metadata, merge-safe install, and uninstall-safe cleanup","description":"## Why
Safe hook ownership and merge semantics are one of the clearest implementation gaps exposed by the pack. The repo already treats hook integrity as critical, so ownership and cleanup rules need their own planned slice.

## What
1. Add a hook-manager design for managed ownership metadata.
2. Define merge-safe writes for install and refresh paths.
3. Define uninstall-safe cleanup that preserves user-managed hook content.","acceptance_criteria":"- Managed-versus-user-owned hook content is explicitly represented.
- Refresh and uninstall semantics preserve user edits by default.
- The design is concrete enough to guide later smoke tests for install/refresh/uninstall flows.","notes":"Anchors: docs/wip/reviews/2026-04-11-omx-adaptation-pack/specs/002-hook-manager.md and adrs/ADR-002-hook-ownership.md.

{"id":"prompt-language-lcq4","title":"Gate result caching: skip re-evaluation of recently-passed gates (TTL: 30s)","description":"Why: Gates like npm test or cargo test take 30-60s. With 5 gates, each task-completed hook takes up to 5 minutes. If the AI makes a small change and re-attempts, all gates re-run even those that already passed. Caching passed gates for a short TTL dramatically reduces gate latency.

What: Add gateEvaluatedAt timestamp to each gate result in gateDiagnostics. In evaluateCompletion, skip re-evaluation of gates that passed within the last N seconds (default 30s). Failed gates are always re-evaluated.

Acceptance criteria:
- Passed gate within TTL: skip evaluation, use cached result
- Failed gate: always re-evaluate
- TTL configurable via PROMPT_LANGUAGE_GATE_CACHE_TTL env var
- Cache miss on first evaluation (no stale false-pass)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:57:30Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:57:30Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-f7jp.4","title":"Run-state v2 and recovery artifacts layered over current session-state","description":"## Why
The pack argues for a richer run-state layout with recovery checkpoints, child indexes, and operator-readable artifacts. That is valuable, but it overlaps existing replayability work and needs a clearly bounded milestone.

## What
1. Define an additive .prompt-language/runs/<run-id>/... layout and manifest surface.
2. Preserve compatibility with current session-state.json expectations while the migration is in flight.
3. Add recovery-oriented artifacts that help restart, inspect, and debug failed runs.","acceptance_criteria":"- The milestone covers run manifests, compatibility boundaries, and recovery artifacts together.
- The scope stays additive over current state semantics rather than requiring full replay infrastructure first.
- Overlap with broader replay/event-log work is explicit.","notes":"Anchors: docs/wip/reviews/2026-04-11-omx-adaptation-pack/specs/003-run-state-and-recovery.md, 04-open-questions-and-risks.md, adrs/ADR-003-run-state-v2.md.
Overlap: prompt-language-zhog.3 covers broader append-only replay/event-log substrate. Keep this bead focused on operator-facing layout, compatibility, and recovery artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:42:55Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:42:55Z","labels":["area:docs","area:runtime","area:tooling","initiative:operator-shell","type:feature"]}

{"id":"prompt-language-qeyz","title":"Write formal grammar spec (EBNF) + railroad diagrams for the DSL","description":"Why: The parser is 773 lines of ad-hoc regex + state machine with no formal grammar spec. This makes it impossible to reason about ambiguities, ensure completeness, communicate the language to contributors, or build alternative tooling (LSP, syntax highlighter, alternative parsers). Every language improvement adds complexity that a formal spec would make manageable.

What: Write an EBNF grammar for the full DSL, validate it against the parser's actual behavior with test cases, generate railroad diagrams, and document edge cases. ~30 productions. The process will likely reveal latent ambiguities requiring parser fixes.

Acceptance criteria:
- EBNF covers all 13 node kinds, all gate forms, all let sources
- Railroad diagrams generated and added to docs/
- Any ambiguities discovered are filed as separate bugs
- New contributors can use the spec to understand parser behavior","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:34Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T01:55:34Z"}

The repo has historical Claude-based evidence and an in-progress Codex parity track, but it does not yet have the smallest comparable OpenCode/Gemma rerun that answers whether prompt-language still shows directional value on a cheaper runner.

## What

2. Keep the subset narrow: gaslighting resistance, scope mismatch, unstated criteria, and inverted gate cases.
3. Record whether the OpenCode/Gemma results agree directionally with the Claude baseline, even if raw pass rates are lower.
4. File follow-up bugs for unsupported behavior or misleading failures instead of calling the surface parity-ready.

## Acceptance criteria
- The rerun scope is explicitly limited to the smallest high-signal subset.
- Results are captured with runner, model, host, command, exit code, and pass/fail summary.
- Outcome is classified as usable-for-cheap-experiments, baseline-only, or not-ready.
- CRM/helpdesk starter workflows stay blocked until this signal exists.","notes":"Plan anchor: docs/evaluation/opencode-gemma-plan.md. This task is about directional signal first, not full parity or the fixed-stack thesis runs.

The bundle leaves open questions around custom types, attachment typing, review storage, version scoping, large binaries, and whether renderers live in core or plugins. Those choices affect architecture boundaries and implementation cost.

## What

2. Decide where review comments/approvals live and whether they are version-scoped.

{"id":"prompt-language-5pej.3","title":"[REVIEW] Codex CLI: analyze deltas versus Claude baseline and file follow-ups","description":"## Why
A raw pile of Codex run logs is not enough. The product question is whether Codex reproduces the same practical conclusions as the existing prompt-language experiment set.

## What
1. Compare Codex results with the current Claude/experiment baseline
2. Classify each difference as one of: expected host/runtime variance, missing Codex feature, flaky/non-deterministic behavior, or prompt-language bug
3. Create follow-up beads for each concrete product gap or regression
4. Summarize whether Codex currently reaches parity, near-parity, or partial parity

## Acceptance criteria
- Delta analysis written up with explicit classifications
- Follow-up issues created for each unresolved gap
- Overall Codex parity verdict stated clearly","notes":"2026-04-11: The quick Codex suite no longer has unresolved scenario deltas in this workspace: npm run eval:smoke:codex:quick passes 27/27. {"id":"prompt-language-b8kq.3","title":"Decision: checkpoint, handoff, and compaction boundary for memory","description":"## Why
The new memory docs make a stronger claim than the existing backlog: checkpoints, resumability, handoff summaries, and compaction are core runtime behavior, not just extra memory writes. That boundary needs a real product decision before implementation fragments.

## What
1. Define what belongs in execution/session state versus durable memory.
2. Define checkpoint, resumability, handoff-summary, and compaction responsibilities.
3. Reconcile this with existing resume-state backlog so the repo does not grow two overlapping checkpoint models.
4. Create implementation follow-up beads only after the boundary is explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"- Clear boundary documented between execution state and durable memory.
- Checkpoint/handoff/compaction responsibilities are explicit.
- Existing related backlog can be mapped to the chosen model.
- Follow-up implementation beads created only where needed.","notes":"Planning anchors: docs/wip/memory/memory-knowledge-positioning.md and docs/wip/memory/memory-scrutiny-and-risks.md.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"decision","assignee":"45ck","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T19:40:23Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:44:44Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-b8kq.2","title":"Markdown knowledge interop: knowledge sources and deterministic section lookup","description":"## Why
The checked-in docs propose `knowledge:` declarations and deterministic `section` lookup so Prompt Language can consume Markdown guidance without pretending that docs are the same thing as runtime state.

## What
1. Define the DSL surface for declaring Markdown knowledge sources.
2. Define deterministic section-addressing semantics.
3. Specify how loaded knowledge becomes visible in status/artifacts so it remains inspectable.
4. Update docs/tests together.

## Out of scope
- Semantic/vector retrieval as a default language behavior.
- Treating Markdown guidance as automatically authoritative policy.","acceptance_criteria":"- `knowledge:` and `section` semantics are documented and accepted into backlog-ready form.
- Inspectability requirements are explicit.
- Parser/runtime/docs/tests are identified for implementation.
- Validation path is explicit before code changes start.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T19:40:22Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T19:40:22Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-b8kq.1","title":"Scoped memory semantics: strict reads, invalidation, transactional writes","description":"## Why
The current shipped memory surface is useful but too flat. The new planning docs call for scoped memory, strict versus optional reads, TTL/invalidation, and transactional writes so the runtime can stay deterministic instead of quietly trusting stale state.

## What
1. Define the concrete syntax and semantics for memory scopes.
2. Add strict/optional read behavior with explicit failure/default rules.
3. Add TTL/invalidation and transactional write timing.
4. Update parser/runtime/docs/tests together so the behavior is inspectable and not just parser sugar.

## Likely touch points
- `src/application/parse-flow.ts`
- memory store/runtime integration
- docs/reference for `remember` / `memory:`
- parser/runtime tests","acceptance_criteria":"- Scoped memory semantics documented and parser-validated.
- Required memory reads fail loudly; optional reads and defaults are explicit.
- TTL/invalidation and write timing rules are specified and covered by tests.
- Validation evidence includes `npm run test` and `npm run ci`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-04-09T19:40:20Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-09T19:40:20Z"}

Duplicate of prompt-language-b8kq.5, which already tracks the wisdom-promotion governance workflow discovered by the zhog.6 alignment pass.

Accepted the bounded-execution milestone in docs/design/bounded-execution-contracts.md, defining repo-aligned contracts, effects, policy, and capability boundaries with explicit static-vs-dynamic checks and retry/resume semantics.

Accepted the replayability milestone in docs/design/replayability-event-log.md, defining the append-only execution ledger, derived snapshots, replay/report tooling, and checkpoint capture/restore boundaries over existing run-state work.

Accepted the compile-time rigor milestone in docs/design/flow-ir-and-static-analysis.md, defining Flow IR, explain/compile paths, stronger static analysis, simulation, and flow tests sequenced after bounded execution and replayability.

Mapped the imported eval/judge framing onto the existing 5vsm backlog in docs/evaluation/eval-judge-vnext-alignment.md, made gaps and duplicates explicit, and filed the one real follow-up as prompt-language-5vsm.10.

Mapped Spec 012 onto the existing b8kq memory roadmap in docs/design/memory-governance-alignment.md, clarified governance vs retrieval/Markdown/runtime boundaries, and filed the narrow missing follow-up as prompt-language-b8kq.5.

Accepted the provider-adapter milestone in docs/design/provider-adapters-shared-flow-ir.md, defining provider adapters as a later layer over shared Flow IR and relating MCP/LSP/playground work as downstream consumers rather than portability drivers.

{"id":"prompt-language-tfwy","title":"[QA] Edge case test matrix for control flow: empty bodies, max iterations, nested limits","description":"## Why
Control flow edge cases (empty foreach lists, while with max=1, foreach with max_advances exceeded, break at top level, continue in non-loop) are handled but not systematically tested. A matrix test ensures coverage of the combinatorial space.

## What
Create advance-flow.edge-cases.test.ts covering:
1. foreach with empty item list — should skip body entirely
2. while with max=1 — should execute body exactly once
3. retry with max=1 — should execute body exactly once, no retry
4. break immediately at loop start — exits without executing body
5. continue at loop end — re-enters without double-executing
6. nested foreach 3 levels deep — correct path indices
7. try with no catch and no finally — body failure marks flow failed
8. if with no else and false condition — advances past block
9. spawn with no variables in scope — child state is empty
10. let with empty string value — stored as empty string, not null
11. Flow with 0 nodes — completes immediately
12. Single-node flow (just a prompt) — normal advancement

## Acceptance criteria
- All 12+ cases have unit tests
- All pass
- Added to CI (npm run test)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:10:54Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:10:54Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-7b1a","title":"Recency-boost capture instructions: add active-capture reminder to flow rendering","description":"## Why
In long conversations (20+ turns), Claude's attention to early system instructions degrades. The capture format instruction gets buried. Adding a brief reminder in the flow visualization (injected every turn) reinforces the instruction at the right moment.

## What
In renderFlow() (render-flow.ts), when a node is in awaiting_capture state, append a one-liner reminder below the flow visualization:

  [Capture active: write response to .prompt-language/vars/{varName} using Write tool]

This costs ~15 tokens per turn when capture is active and ensures the instruction appears at the end of context (most attended by the model).

Key files: src/domain/render-flow.ts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Reminder line appears in render output when capture is active
- Reminder correctly shows variable name
- Only shown when awaiting_capture (not on every turn)
- Unit tests for render output with/without active capture","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:04:11Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:04:11Z"}

OpenClaw is the most interesting non-Claude host in the current research set because it has durable agents, plugins, approvals, and a more always-on operational model. An integration spike would test whether prompt-language should remain session-scoped, become host-adaptable, or stop at advisory integration.

## What
1. Identify the smallest credible OpenClaw integration path: wrapper flow runner, plugin, prompt asset, or host-interop shim

3. Define the security posture for any integration proposal, especially around skills/plugins, approvals, and secret handling
4. Produce a recommendation: prototype now, track as future work, or defer

## Acceptance criteria
- Integration options and constraints documented
- Security and product-boundary implications called out explicitly
- Clear next-step recommendation produced","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-28T23:15:05Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-28T23:15:05Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-tfwy","title":"[QA] Edge case test matrix for control flow: empty bodies, max iterations, nested limits","description":"## Why
Control flow edge cases (empty foreach lists, while with max=1, foreach with max_advances exceeded, break at top level, continue in non-loop) are handled but not systematically tested. A matrix test ensures coverage of the combinatorial space.

## What
Create advance-flow.edge-cases.test.ts covering:
1. foreach with empty item list — should skip body entirely
2. while with max=1 — should execute body exactly once
3. retry with max=1 — should execute body exactly once, no retry
4. break immediately at loop start — exits without executing body
5. continue at loop end — re-enters without double-executing
6. nested foreach 3 levels deep — correct path indices
7. try with no catch and no finally — body failure marks flow failed
8. if with no else and false condition — advances past block
9. spawn with no variables in scope — child state is empty
10. let with empty string value — stored as empty string, not null
11. Flow with 0 nodes — completes immediately
12. Single-node flow (just a prompt) — normal advancement

## Acceptance criteria
- All 12+ cases have unit tests
- All pass
- Added to CI (npm run test)","notes":"2026-04-11: Claimed for multi-agent QA wave; bounded to one new edge-case test file and related assertions only.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:10:54Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:29:30Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-yqby","title":"[REVIEW] Security audit: injection, credentials, nonce, and trust boundary review","description":"## Why
The security model has multiple safeguards (shell injection, capture injection, credential filtering, path traversal, state integrity). New features can introduce gaps. Periodic review ensures no safeguard has been silently weakened.

## What (recurring — run before each release)
1. Review shellInterpolate() — are all run: command substitutions using it?
2. Review filterSpawnVariables() — does it still exclude _key/_token/_secret/_password?
3. Review the path traversal regex in evaluate-completion.ts — still correct?
4. Review the capture nonce — still UUID4, per-session, validated on read?
5. Check for any new user-controlled strings that reach shell execution
6. Run npm audit — any high/critical vulnerabilities?
7. Review file_exists gate: still using the allowlist + safe regex?
8. Check output truncation limits — still applied at all injection points?

## Acceptance criteria
- npm audit shows 0 high/critical issues
- No new unprotected shell interpolation paths found
- All credential filter patterns still active
- Findings documented as comment

## Recurrence
Close and create a new instance before each release.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:09:23Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:09:23Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-cva6","title":"Add const keyword for immutable variable declarations","description":"## Why
Variables set once (API keys, config values, loop limits) can be accidentally overwritten by Claude in a loop body. A const keyword prevents reassignment and documents intent.

## What
Add const as a third alias alongside let/var. A const-declared variable cannot be reassigned. In updateVariable() (session-state.ts), check if the variable was declared const and emit a warning (or soft error) on reassignment attempt.

The parser recognizes 'const' in the same position as 'let'/'var'. LetNode gains a isConst: boolean field.

Key files: src/domain/flow-node.ts (LetNode), src/application/parse-flow.ts (parseLetLine), src/domain/session-state.ts (updateVariable).

## Acceptance criteria
- const parsed identically to let/var
- Reassignment of const warns at runtime
- Linter warns on const reassignment statically
- const included in flow rendering (shown as 'const' not 'let')
- Backward compatible","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:28Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:28Z"}

The post-tool-use hook currently only scans toolOutput for capture tags. Claude sometimes emits the capture tag content in its regular text response rather than (or in addition to) tool output. This valid capture attempt is silently ignored, causing unnecessary retry.

## What

If both have values, prefer the file-write channel (more structured). This is a low-cost extension of the existing multi-channel approach.

Key files: src/presentation/hooks/post-tool-use.ts (scanAndSaveCapturedVars).

- Response text scanned for capture tags alongside tool output

- No regression on existing capture tests","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:34Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:34Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-nm4l","title":"Add variable shadow warnings to linter for foreach and nested scopes","description":"## Why
Variables in prompt-language are global. A foreach iterator variable named 'item' inside a foreach 'item' silently shadows the outer variable. This causes subtle bugs that are invisible at runtime — the outer loop's iterator gets corrupted. The linter can catch this statically.

## What
In lintFlow() / lintNodes(), track a scope stack as the linter descends into loop bodies and branches. When a let or foreach declares a variable name that already exists in an outer scope, emit a warning: 'Variable X shadows outer definition in foreach/while/if'.

Also: after foreach body exhaustion (handleBodyExhaustion), clean up the foreach iterator variable from SessionState to prevent stale values leaking after the loop.

Key files: src/domain/lint-flow.ts, src/application/advance-flow.ts (handleBodyExhaustion).

## Acceptance criteria
- Linter warns on variable shadowing in nested scopes
- Warning message includes variable name and scope type
- Foreach iterator cleaned from state after loop completes
- No false positives on non-shadowing variable reuse","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:46Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:46Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-rr6a","title":"Add gradual typing for let declarations (let x: int = 0)","description":"## Why
All variables are stringly-typed. The condition evaluator does implicit numeric coercion — if count is 'pending', ${count} > 5 silently returns null and falls through to command execution. Type annotations let the parser and linter catch these mismatches statically.

## What
Add optional type annotations to let/var declarations:
  let count: int = 0
  let name: string = 'alice'
  let done: bool = false
  let items: list = []

Parse the ': type' after variable name in parseLetLine(). Store declaredType on LetNode. At runtime in updateVariable(), validate assigned value matches declared type (warn on mismatch, don't hard-fail for backward compat). In the linter, warn when comparison operators are used on string-typed variables, or when a list variable is used in a numeric comparison.

Key files: src/domain/flow-node.ts (LetNode), src/application/parse-flow.ts (parseLetLine), src/domain/session-state.ts (updateVariable), src/domain/lint-flow.ts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Type annotations parsed and stored on LetNode
- Runtime validation warns on type mismatch
- Linter warns on comparison operators with declared string type
- Fully backward compatible (untyped variables behave as before)
- Unit tests for parsing and validation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:14Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:14Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-nwa0","title":"Add runtime invariant assertions to catch state machine corruption early","description":"## Why
State corruption manifests as bizarre behavior far from the corruption site. Runtime invariant checks after each state transition would catch corruption immediately when it happens, with a useful error message.

## What
Implement assertStateInvariants(state: SessionState): void as a pure domain function. Invariants to check:
1. path resolves to a valid node or is one-past-end
2. nodeProgress iteration <= maxIterations for all nodes
3. status === 'active' implies path.length >= 1
4. setExitVariables values are consistent (command_failed != command_succeeded)
5. transitionSeq is present and non-negative
6. No variable name contains the capture nonce substring

Call assertStateInvariants() in autoAdvanceNodes() after each node transition. In production: log violations. In test (NODE_ENV=test): throw.

Key files: src/domain/session-state.ts or new src/domain/assert-invariants.ts, src/application/advance-flow.ts.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pure domain function with zero external deps
- All 6 invariants checked
- Called after each transition in autoAdvanceNodes
- Throws in test environment, logs in production
- Unit tests for each invariant violation scenario","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:01Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:01Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-1wr7.3","title":"Lower `swarm` to spawn/await/send/receive over the existing runtime","description":"## Why
The strongest architectural claim in the pack is that swarm should lower to existing primitives instead of creating a second execution engine. That lowering needs to be a first-class implementation slice.

## What
1. Desugar `start` to `spawn` and `return` to child-to-parent message transport.
2. Lower `await <role>` and `await all` into explicit join/receive behavior.
3. Make the expanded form inspectable so debugging and smoke tests stay grounded in the real runtime.","acceptance_criteria":"- Swarm lowering is explicit and inspectable.
- The lowered execution is equivalent to a hand-written spawn/await/send/receive flow.

{"id":"prompt-language-nm4l","title":"Add variable shadow warnings to linter for foreach and nested scopes","description":"## Why
Variables in prompt-language are global. A foreach iterator variable named 'item' inside a foreach 'item' silently shadows the outer variable. This causes subtle bugs that are invisible at runtime — the outer loop's iterator gets corrupted. The linter can catch this statically.

## What
In lintFlow() / lintNodes(), track a scope stack as the linter descends into loop bodies and branches. When a let or foreach declares a variable name that already exists in an outer scope, emit a warning: 'Variable X shadows outer definition in foreach/while/if'.

Also: after foreach body exhaustion (handleBodyExhaustion), clean up the foreach iterator variable from SessionState to prevent stale values leaking after the loop.

Key files: src/domain/lint-flow.ts, src/application/advance-flow.ts (handleBodyExhaustion).

## Acceptance criteria
- Linter warns on variable shadowing in nested scopes
- Warning message includes variable name and scope type
- Foreach iterator cleaned from state after loop completes
- No false positives on non-shadowing variable reuse","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"45ck","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:46Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T20:25:27Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-l1jv","title":"Scan full Claude response text for capture tags (not just tool output)","description":"## Why
The post-tool-use hook currently only scans toolOutput for capture tags. Claude sometimes emits the capture tag content in its regular text response rather than (or in addition to) tool output. This valid capture attempt is silently ignored, causing unnecessary retry.

## What
In post-tool-use.ts scanAndSaveCapturedVars(), also scan the response_text field (if present in stdin JSON) for capture tag patterns. Accept capture from either channel. If both have values, prefer the file-write channel (more structured). This is a low-cost extension of the existing multi-channel approach.

Key files: src/presentation/hooks/post-tool-use.ts (scanAndSaveCapturedVars).

## Acceptance criteria
- Response text scanned for capture tags alongside tool output
- Both channels checked, file-write takes precedence
- No regression on existing capture tests","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"45ck","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:01:34Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:28:25Z"}

{"id":"prompt-language-cva6","title":"Add const keyword for immutable variable declarations","description":"## Why
Variables set once (API keys, config values, loop limits) can be accidentally overwritten by Claude in a loop body. A const keyword prevents reassignment and documents intent.

## What
Add const as a third alias alongside let/var. A const-declared variable cannot be reassigned. In updateVariable() (session-state.ts), check if the variable was declared const and emit a warning (or soft error) on reassignment attempt.

The parser recognizes 'const' in the same position as 'let'/'var'. LetNode gains a isConst: boolean field.

Key files: src/domain/flow-node.ts (LetNode), src/application/parse-flow.ts (parseLetLine), src/domain/session-state.ts (updateVariable).

## Acceptance criteria
- const parsed identically to let/var
- Reassignment of const warns at runtime
- Linter warns on const reassignment statically
- const included in flow rendering (shown as 'const' not 'let')
- Backward compatible","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"45ck","owner":"98618920+45ck@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:02:28Z","created_by":"45ck","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:28:25Z"}

Implemented dry-run/validate gate-check execution path with pass/fail outputs and standalone behavior tests.
Added /flow-eval command+skill with traced condition evaluation, last-session fallback, and contract/docs/tests updates.

{"id":"prompt-language-ksih.1","title":"E4 method reset: claim families and factory-quality rubric","description":"## Why
The current framework still over-weights time-to-green. E4 needs first-class claim families so prompt-language can be judged on governed SDLC quality and recovery, not just speed.
